questions.question: Explain the purpose of the /bin, /home, and /etc directories in the Unix filesystem.
expectedPoints: /bin contains essential user command binaries (e.g., ls, cp) available to all users.
/home is the location for user home directories, where personal files and configurations are stored.
/etc contains host-specific system configuration files for the operating system and applications (e.g., passwd file, network settings).

questions.question: Compare and contrast absolute and relative pathnames. Provide examples of each and explain when you would use one over the other.
expectedPoints: Definition: Absolute path begins from root (/), relative path begins from current working directory.
Example: Absolute: /home/user/file.txt. Relative: if in /home, path is user/file.txt.
Use of Special Symbols: Relative paths use . for current dir and .. for parent dir.
Uniqueness: Absolute path always points to the same file. Relative path changes meaning based on current directory.
When to Use: Absolute paths are used in scripts for reliability. Relative paths are often faster to type for navigation within a project's directory tree.
